title: Add production L2 raw announce dispatch to gateway announce ingestion
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-r9bb added a software-only C announce parser/verifier/ingestion boundary, but the production L2 receive path currently assumes every authenticated LICHEN frame payload is SCHC-compressed IPv6 and injects it via net_recv_data(). Raw spec §9.2 announce routing messages are not represented in the C L2/routing dispatch path, so no RF/native_sim receive hook can honestly call gateway_announce_ingest_verified() yet. What needs to be done: define how raw announce routing payloads are distinguished from SCHC IPv6 payloads in C, add a production dispatch point after authentication and before/alongside IPv6 injection, update C TOFU/peer/gradient routing state, call gateway_announce_ingest_verified() for location app_data, and cover native_sim/qemu injection. Blocked until the C link/L2 payload demux and gradient table ownership are decided.
acceptance_criteria: No-hardware native_sim/qemu tests inject signed announce bytes through the production L2/routing receive path and prove authenticated announces update peer/gradient state and gateway network-location ingestion. RF hardware validation is chained separately.
notes: Blocked after no-hardware investigation for an unsafe protocol/layering gap, not hardware. Evidence: raw announce Type=0x01 in lichen/apps/gateway/src/announce_ingest.h collides with SCHC_RULE_GLOBAL_COAP=1 in lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/include/lichen/schc.h, and canonical test/vectors/schc_compression.json coap_global compressed bytes begin with 0x01. lichen_link_rx() currently authenticates/MIC-verifies then immediately SCHC-decompresses at lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_rx.c, so lichen_l2_input() never receives authenticated raw inner payload bytes or link epoch/seq metadata for a safe raw announce dispatch. lichen/subsys/lichen/l2 is a reusable subsystem and must not directly call app-private lichen/apps/gateway/src/announce_ingest.c. Three independent subagents confirmed: Curie found first-byte demux unsafe; Copernicus mapped the authenticated pre-SCHC insertion point and metadata/layering constraints; Hypatia identified ping_l2 as the eventual production-path native_sim test surface once the API/discriminator exist. Created blockers: project-LICHEN-l812 for explicit payload discriminator, project-LICHEN-ct4d for authenticated raw link payload RX API, and project-LICHEN-8ko4 for neutral announce routing/gradient ownership between L2 and gateway app. No code changed for this Bead because the production dispatch would be ambiguous without those blockers.
